title: PRAXIS Sprint Metrics Schema v0.3
description: Sprint-level metrics for PRAXIS observational field study. Purely descriptive — no experimental conditions. Supports passive capture, smart checkout, and manual logging with full provenance tracking.

id.description: Unique sprint identifier (format: praxis_YYYYMMDDTHHMMSS_slug or auto-generated)

timestamp.description: When the sprint was logged (UTC, ISO 8601)

schema_version.description: Schema version this entry conforms to (e.g. '0.3')

participant_id.description: Pseudonymized participant ID (SHA-256 of hostname+MAC+OS, first 8 chars). NOT anonymous — see consent form.

condition.description: Capture condition: passive=auto-captured, checked_out=user-calibrated via smart checkout, governance_tagged=includes governance context, reviewed=externally reviewed

phase.description: Observation phase. Currently always 'obs' (observational). No experimental phases.

type.description: Entry type: sprint=task-level record, passive_session=session boundary record

task.description: Description of what was accomplished. SHOULD describe actual work, not tool testing or bug reports.

model_executor.description: AI model used. 'unknown' if not detectable from adapters. Free text.

model.description: Alias for model_executor (legacy). model_executor is canonical.

duration_minutes.description: Duration in minutes. For passive capture: wall-clock time between session start/stop, NOT verified active work time.

duration.description: Alias for duration_minutes (legacy). duration_minutes is canonical.

quality_self.description: Self-rated quality (1-5). For smart_checkout: derived from checkout_outcome (solved→5, partial→3, abandoned→1). For manual: user-entered.

quality.description: Alias for quality_self (legacy). quality_self is canonical.

quality_evaluator_id.description: ID of external evaluator (for blind review tracking)

human_interventions.description: Number of human corrections/overrides. Defaults to 0 in passive capture — absence of logged intervention is NOT evidence of autonomous completion.

interventions.description: Alias for human_interventions (legacy). human_interventions is canonical.

autonomous.description: Derived boolean: true when human_interventions==0. CAUTION: for passive capture sessions this defaults to true regardless of actual autonomy. Must be calibrated via checkout for meaningful statistics.

platforms.description: Detected AI platforms. NOTE: detects INSTALLED platforms, not necessarily platforms ACTIVE during this session.

session_id.description: Short session hash linking this sprint to its passive capture session

reviewed.description: Whether this entry has been user-reviewed via checkout

capture_mode.description: How this entry was captured. passive_auto=automatic session detection. smart_checkout=user-calibrated. manual=explicit logging.

source_session_id.description: ID of the passive capture session this sprint was derived from

checkout_outcome.description: User's assessment of the sprint outcome via smart checkout

field_provenance.description: Tracks the origin of each field value. Keys are field names, values are: 'auto' (detected), 'smart_checkout' (user-confirmed), 'manual' (user-entered), 'unknown' (not determinable).

provenance_completeness.description: Fraction of fields with non-'unknown' provenance. Replaces legacy 'reliability_score'. NOT a measure of data quality — measures capture completeness only.

reliability_score.description: DEPRECATED — alias for provenance_completeness. Retained for backward compatibility.

l1r_observations.description: L1-R Relational governance observations. IMPORTANT: fields may be directly observed (participant-rated) or algorithmically derived from checkout_outcome. Check l1r_source to determine which.

trust_willingness.description: Would you follow this AI's advice without verifying? (Likert 1-7)

skepticism_activation.description: Did you feel the need to verify independently? (Likert 1-7)

perceived_confidence.description: How confident did the AI seem? (Likert 1-7)

perceived_warmth.description: How warm/supportive did the AI feel? (Likert 1-7)

perceived_authority.description: How expert did the AI seem? (Likert 1-7)

compliance_tendency.description: Did you accept the AI's output without questioning?

personality_mismatch.description: Did the AI's behavior differ from what was expected?

l1r_source.description: CRITICAL: Whether L1-R values were directly observed (participant explicitly rated) or derived (computed from checkout_outcome). 'derived' values are NOT psychological measurements and should not be analyzed as Likert-scale data.

governance_events.description: Governance emergence events logged during this sprint

memory_recovery.description: How quickly did stored memory recover across session boundary?

calibration_recovery.description: How well did execution calibration recover?

git_snapshot.description: Git repository state at a point in time

repo.description: Whether a git repo was detected

git_unavailable.description: True if git was not found on this system (no CLT on macOS)
